title: strychnos innocua subsp. burtonii
intro: Strychnos innocua subsp. burtonii, commonly known as the African strychnine tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its toxic seeds and medicinal properties. The plant is used for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Traditional herbal preparations of Strychnos innocua subsp. burtonii include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to alleviate symptoms of various ailments and promote overall health. However, due to its toxicity, the plant should be handled with caution, and its use should be guided by experienced practitioners.
